ppt.title: Photosynthesis: The Food Factory
ppt.presentationGoal: Explain how plants convert light energy into chemical food.

licensingNotes: Use internally generated images and in-app SVG diagrams only.
assetSearchPlan.fallbackStrategy: Prefer SVG diagrams for concept/process slides and the Ollama image model for all picture-based visuals.

licenseChecklist: Review generated visuals for classroom accuracy before final export.
presentationWarnings: Do not include untaught future-session content.

learningOutcomesCovered: Identify inputs and outputs of photosynthesis

slides.teacherIntent: Introduce the topic and set the context for the lesson.
slides.studentTakeaway: Understand that plants make their own food using sunlight.
onSlideText: How do plants make their own food?
speakerNotes: Welcome the class and introduce the topic.
Ask students: 'Where does our food come from?'
Transition to the idea that plants are unique because they create food.
slides.visualPlan: A high-quality image of a vibrant green leaf with sunlight rays hitting it.

assets.searchQuery: Green leaf close up with sunlight rays nature
assets.altText: Close-up of a green leaf illuminated by sunlight

animationHints: Fade in title and leaf image

slides.teacherIntent: Set clear expectations for student achievement.
slides.studentTakeaway: Know exactly what they will be able to do by the end of class.
speakerNotes: Read the outcomes aloud.
Explain that by the end, they will be able to write the chemical equation.
Connect these goals to real life: 'Why do plants need sunlight?'
slides.visualPlan: Simple icons representing a sun, water drop, and leaf.

assets.searchQuery: Sun icon water drop icon leaf icon flat design
assets.altText: Icons representing sun, water, and a leaf

animationHints: Slide in icons one by one

slides.teacherIntent: Activate prior knowledge about plants and food.
slides.studentTakeaway: Connect existing knowledge to the new concept.
bulletPoints: Do all living things make food?
What do animals eat for energy?
Where do plants get their energy?
onSlideText: Think: How do plants survive without eating?
speakerNotes: Ask students to think-pair-share.
Clarify that animals eat food, but plants make it.
Bridge to the concept of 'autotrophs'.
slides.visualPlan: A simple illustration comparing a human eating an apple vs. a plant standing in the sun.

assets.searchQuery: Cartoon human eating apple vs plant growing in sun
assets.altText: Comparison of a person eating and a plant growing

animationHints: Reveal question text first, then image

slides.slideTitle: The Mystery of the Green Leaf
slides.teacherIntent: Capture attention with a surprising fact or question.
slides.studentTakeaway: Realize that plants are like solar-powered factories.
bulletPoints: They don't go to the grocery store.
So how do they get food?
onSlideText: How does a plant eat without a mouth?
speakerNotes: Use this hook to spark curiosity.
Explain that plants use sunlight to cook their own food.
Transition to the specific process: Photosynthesis.
slides.visualPlan: A close-up of a green leaf with a 'factory' icon overlaid or implied.

assets.searchQuery: Green leaf with factory gears concept art
assets.altText: Artistic representation of a leaf as a food factory

slides.teacherIntent: Define the term and explain its importance.
slides.studentTakeaway: Understand that photosynthesis is making food using light.
speakerNotes: Break down the word: Photo (light) + Synthesis (making).
Explain that this process happens in leaves.
Emphasize that this is how plants survive.
slides.visualPlan: A diagram showing a leaf with arrows pointing in (sun, water, CO2) and out (food, oxygen).

assets.searchQuery: Leaf diagram showing sunlight water and carbon dioxide inputs
assets.altText: Diagram of a leaf with input and output arrows

svgDiagram.title: Photosynthesis Inputs
instructions: Show arrows entering a leaf labeled Sunlight, Water, CO2.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><rect x='50' y='50' width='100' height='100' fill='#36ADAA'/><line x1='100' y1='50' x2='150' y2='20' stroke='black'/><text x='140' y='15'>Sun</text></svg>

animationHints: Animate arrows moving into the leaf

slides.teacherIntent: List the specific materials plants need.
slides.studentTakeaway: Identify sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide as inputs.
speakerNotes: Explain where each ingredient comes from.
Water comes through roots; CO2 enters through tiny pores (stomata).
Sunlight provides the energy to cook.
slides.visualPlan: Three distinct icons or photos representing Sun, Water Drop, and Gas Cloud.

assets.searchQuery: Sun icon water drop icon gas cloud icon set
assets.altText: Icons representing sun, water, and gas

svgDiagram.title: Inputs Diagram
instructions: Show three distinct boxes for Sunlight, Water, CO2.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><rect x='10' y='10' width='80' height='80' fill='#DE8431'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='80' height='80' fill='#1EABDA'/><rect x='190' y='10' width='80' height='80' fill='#64748B'/></svg>

animationHints: Fade in each ingredient one by one

slides.teacherIntent: Explain the role of chlorophyll in capturing light.
slides.studentTakeaway: Understand that chlorophyll is the 'chef' that captures sunlight.
speakerNotes: Ask why leaves are green.
Explain that chlorophyll absorbs light and reflects green.
Use the analogy: Chlorophyll is like a solar panel.
slides.visualPlan: A microscopic view or diagram of a leaf cell showing chloroplasts (green circles).

assets.searchQuery: Microscopic view of plant cells with green chloroplasts
assets.altText: Microscopic view of plant cells showing green chloroplasts

svgDiagram.title: Chloroplast Structure
instructions: Draw a circle representing a cell with smaller green circles inside.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40' fill='#36ADAA'/><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='10' fill='#DE8431'/><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='10' fill='#DE8431'/></svg>

animationHints: Zoom into the cell to show chloroplasts

slides.studentTakeaway: Memorize and understand the inputs/outputs relationship.
speakerNotes: Write the equation on the board.
Explain that CO2 and Water are reactants.
Glucose is food; Oxygen is a byproduct we breathe.
slides.visualPlan: A balanced chemical equation with arrows showing direction.

assets.searchQuery: Chemical equation diagram CO2 H2O Glucose Oxygen
assets.altText: Diagram of the photosynthesis chemical equation

svgDiagram.title: Equation Flow
instructions: Show CO2 and H2O entering, arrow with Sunlight, Glucose and O2 leaving.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><text x='10' y='30'>CO2 + H2O</text><line x1='50' y1='30' x2='90' y2='30' stroke='black'/><text x='95' y='30'>Sunlight</text><line x1='110' y1='30' x2='150' y2='30' stroke='black'/><text x='155' y='30'>Glucose + O2</text></svg>

animationHints: Animate the arrow moving from left to right

slides.teacherIntent: Engage students in completing a partial equation.
slides.studentTakeaway: Apply knowledge to fill in missing parts of the process.
speakerNotes: Ask students to shout out the answers.
Guide them to fill in CO2, Water, Glucose, Oxygen.
Correct any misconceptions immediately.
slides.visualPlan: A blank equation template with question marks or empty boxes.

assets.altText: Blank chemical equation template for filling in

svgDiagram.title: Fill the Blanks
instructions: Show empty boxes for inputs and outputs.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><rect x='10' y='10' width='50' height='20' fill='#eee'/><text x='70' y='30'>+</text><rect x='90' y='10' width='50' height='20' fill='#eee'/></svg>

animationHints: Reveal answer boxes one by one

slides.studentTakeaway: Self-assess their understanding of the lesson.
bulletPoints: What gas do plants take in?
speakerNotes: Ask students to raise hands or use thumbs up/down.
Review answers: CO2, Chlorophyll, Glucose.
Address any lingering confusion.
slides.visualPlan: Three question marks with icons for Gas, Color, Food.

assets.searchQuery: Question mark icon gas cloud green leaf apple
assets.altText: Icons representing questions about gas, color, and food

svgDiagram.title: Assessment Icons
instructions: Show three icons for the three questions.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='15' fill='#DE8431'/><circle cx='60' cy='20' r='15' fill='#36ADAA'/><circle cx='100' cy='20' r='15' fill='#1EABDA'/></svg>

slides.teacherIntent: Reinforce the main points of the lesson.
slides.studentTakeaway: Recall the definition, inputs, and equation.
speakerNotes: Review the three main points.
Ask students to repeat the equation aloud.
Connect back to the 'Food Factory' analogy.
slides.visualPlan: A summary diagram showing the whole cycle from sun to food.

assets.searchQuery: Photosynthesis cycle diagram sun leaf oxygen

svgDiagram.title: Cycle Summary
instructions: Show a loop from Sun to Plant to Food.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='30' fill='#DE8431'/><line x1='80' y1='50' x2='120' y2='50' stroke='black'/><circle cx='130' cy='50' r='30' fill='#36ADAA'/></svg>

slides.teacherIntent: Assign tasks and preview the next lesson.
slides.studentTakeaway: Know what to do for homework and what's coming next.
bulletPoints: Draw a labeled diagram of photosynthesis.
Next: How do plants get water?
speakerNotes: Explain the homework assignment clearly.
Encourage observation of real plants.
Preview that next class will focus on water transport.
slides.visualPlan: An image of a notebook with a drawing and a garden scene.

assets.purpose: Visual representation of homework and observation
assets.searchQuery: Notebook with plant drawing and garden background
assets.altText: Image of a notebook with a plant drawing and a garden

svgDiagram.title: Homework Icon
instructions: Show a simple icon of a notebook.
svgDiagram.svgCode: <svg><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='80' fill='#eee'/><line x1='20' y1='30' x2='60' y2='30' stroke='#ccc'/></svg>

pdf.documentTitle: Photosynthesis: The Food Factory - Student Notes
keyInformation: Plants make food using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide.
Chlorophyll in leaves captures light energy.

sections: Introduction to Photosynthesis and its importance.
Definition and breakdown of the term.
Inputs required: Sunlight, Water, Carbon Dioxide.
Role of Chlorophyll in capturing light.
The Chemical Equation and its components.
Homework assignment and next session preview.
